task_completion.description: Open the 'More actions' menu in the Jitsi Meet interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'More actions' menu (usually accessed via a three-dots icon on the bottom toolbar) is expanded and visible on the screen.
A modal window titled 'Settings' (or similar) is open in the center of the Jitsi Meet interface.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the language selection options within the settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown menu specifically for selecting the interface language is visible within the Settings dialog (often located under a 'More' tab).

task_completion.description: Select French as the target language.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The language dropdown shows 'French' or 'Français' as the currently highlighted or selected option.

task_completion.description: Verify the Jitsi Meet interface is displayed in French.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Jitsi Meet interface, toolbar buttons, menus, or tooltips display French text (e.g., 'Plus d'actions', 'Quitter', 'Paramètres', 'Discuter').

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Jitsi Meet interface via a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window is visible, showing the Jitsi Meet application running on http://localhost:8080.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to manipulate the DOM or local storage directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspector, Console, Application tabs) are not open in any of the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface rather than terminal commands to change the language.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing scripts or commands (like sqlite3 or jq) being used to directly modify Firefox's localStorage files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Jitsi Meet instance is running locally on http://localhost:8080. The target language is French (ISO 639-1 code 'fr'). When successfully changed, the interface will display French terminology (e.g., 'Français' in the language dropdown, and French labels on the main interface toolbars and menus). The language preference is technically stored in Firefox's localStorage under the key 'features/base/settings', but the agent is expected to change this via the Jitsi Meet graphical user interface.
